metadata.summary: Read and modify parameters used in neuroscientific in silico circuit building.

documentation:
# fz-td-recipe

A wrapper around a connectome building recipe used by BlueBrain tools, to be either
defined in JSON/YAML or XML (deprecated).

## Installation

To install the latest stable release, please use:

```console
$ pip install fz-td-recipe
```

Or to get the latest code, build it from `git`:

```console
$ gh repo clone BlueBrain/fz-td-recipe
$ cd fz-td-recipe
$ pip install .
```

## Usage

A small command line utility is provided to convert legacy XML recipes and validate
whether a JSON or YAML file conforms to the recipe schema:

```console
$ fz-td-recipe convert recipe.xml recipe.json
$ fz-td-recipe validate recipe.json
```

More details and features can be accessed with i.e.:

```console
$ fz-td-recipe validate --help
```

## Further Documentation

The recipe format is documented within the [SONATA extension] by the Blue Brain Project.

## Acknowledgment

The development of this software was supported by funding to the Blue Brain Project,
a research center of the École polytechnique fédérale de Lausanne (EPFL),
from the Swiss government's ETH Board of the Swiss Federal Institutes of Technology.

Copyright (c) 2021-2024 Blue Brain Project/EPFL

[sonata extension]: https://sonata-extension.readthedocs.io/en/latest/recipe.html

modules.name: fz_td_recipe.recipe
documentation: Top-level interface for recipe handling.

classes.name: fz_td_recipe.recipe.Recipe

methods.name: fz_td_recipe.recipe.Recipe.__init__
documentation:
Open a recipe in `filename` using the corresponding circuit config.

The `filename` argument may point either to a JSON file ending in `.json` or a
YAML file ending in either `.yml` or `.yaml`.

methods.name: fz_td_recipe.recipe.Recipe.get
documentation:
Get a recipe component under `key`.

Nested values are accessed by joining all keys with a period.

methods.name: fz_td_recipe.recipe.Recipe.get_values
documentation:
Return a dictionary with all values of the specified columns.

Dictionary keys are the column names, values are taken from the corresponding node
population of the circuit configuration passed to the constructor.

methods.name: fz_td_recipe.recipe.Recipe.set
documentation:
Set a recipe component under `key`.

Nested values are set by joining all keys with a period.

methods.name: fz_td_recipe.recipe.Recipe.as_matrix
documentation:
Construct a rule assignment matrix.

Takes a list of rules, determines which columns relating to the source and target
population are of essence (contain more than just general wildcards `*`).  The
enumeration value count in the corresponding node population `@libray` enumeration
then is used to construct a matrix, where the entries in the matrix set depending
on the numerical values of the columns, with wildcards expanded.

Returns a tuple containing the list of columns used (ordered corresponding to the
matrix dimensions) and the matrix itself.

methods.name: fz_td_recipe.recipe.Recipe.as_pandas
documentation:
Converts the rules in `component` to a Pandas DataFrame.

All partial wildcards will be expanded, and columns relating to source or target
population will be converted to numerical values corresponding to the `@library`
enumeration entries in the node populations.
Wrapper to load and validate a JSON or YAML recipe.

Create a new touch rule.

Enforces that the deprecated attributes `fromLayer` and `toLayer` are set to
`*`, and raises a `ValueError` if not.

methods.name: fz_td_recipe.parts.touch_rules.TouchRule.__call__
documentation:
Expands the rule for the given mtypes.

Will yield one tuple for every possible combination, containing:

* source mtype
* target mtype
* source branch types allowed
* target branch types allowed
Class representing a Touch rule.

classes.name: fz_td_recipe.parts.touch_rules.TouchRules

methods.name: fz_td_recipe.parts.touch_rules.TouchRules.to_matrix
documentation:
Construct a touch rule matrix.

Args:
    src_mtypes: The morphology types associated with the source population
    dst_mtypes: The morphology types associated with the target population
Returns:
    A multidimensional matrix, containing a one (1) for every
    connection allowed. The dimensions correspond to the numeical
    indices of morphology types of source and destination, as well
    as the rule type.
A collection of `TouchRule` elements.

modules.name: fz_td_recipe
documentation:
Read and modify HPC circuit building recipes.

This module provides the {class}`XMLRecipe` class to handle recipe reading and writing. A
short example to read a recipe and modify the initial bouton distance for inhibitory
synapses:

```pycon
>>> from fz_td_recipe import XMLRecipe
>>> r = XMLRecipe("tests/data/v7.xml")
>>> print(r.bouton_distances)
<InitialBoutonDistance inhibitorySynapsesDistance="5.0" excitatorySynapsesDistance="25.0" />
>>> r.bouton_distances.inhibitorySynapsesDistance = 4.5
```

To write the recipe into a single file, containing all parts:

```pycon
>>> with open("one_file.xml", "w") as fd:
...     r.dump(fd)
```

And to produce a "classical" split, where the connectivity part of the
recipe is written to a different file:

```pycon
>>> with open("recipe.xml", "w") as f1, open("connectivitiy.xml", "w") as f2:
...     r.dump(f1, connectivity_fd=f2)
```

modules.name: fz_td_recipe.parts.synapse_properties
documentation: Specification of synaptic properties and classification.

classes.name: fz_td_recipe.parts.synapse_properties.SynapseRule

methods.name: fz_td_recipe.parts.synapse_properties.SynapseRule.__init__
documentation:
Creates a new synapse rule.

Enforces that the classification starts with either `E` or `I`, for exitatory
or inhibitory synapses, respectively.
Class representing a Synapse property.

classes.name: fz_td_recipe.parts.synapse_properties.SynapseRules
documentation: A group of `SynapseRule`.

classes.name: fz_td_recipe.parts.synapse_properties.SynapseClass

documentation:
Extract the synapse properties from `xml`.

Enforces that the attributes `gsynSRSF` and `uHillCoefficient` are set for
either none or all of the properties.
Container for synaptic properties per class.

classes.name: fz_td_recipe.parts.synapse_properties.RuleMismatch

methods.name: fz_td_recipe.parts.synapse_properties.RuleMismatch.__init__
documentation:
Create a new exception.

Args:
    duplicated: rules that occur more than once
    mismatch: rules that have an identifier that is not matched by other rules

methods.name: fz_td_recipe.parts.synapse_properties.RuleMismatch.__reduce__
documentation: Allows to pickle exceptions.
Exception to handle mismatches between rules.

classes.name: fz_td_recipe.parts.synapse_properties.SynapseProperties

methods.name: fz_td_recipe.parts.synapse_properties.SynapseProperties.__init__
documentation: Create a new container.

methods.name: fz_td_recipe.parts.synapse_properties.SynapseProperties.__str__
documentation: An XML like representation of the properties.

methods.name: fz_td_recipe.parts.synapse_properties.SynapseProperties.validate
documentation:
Validate synapse properties.

As rule mismatch is already handled at loading time, this function always returns
`True`.

methods.name: fz_td_recipe.parts.synapse_properties.SynapseProperties.load
documentation:
Extract synapes classification and properties from `xml`.

Will check if any of the classification rules and property rules are unmatched or
duplicated.

methods.name: fz_td_recipe.parts.synapse_properties.SynapseProperties._duplicated
documentation: Yields rules that are present more than once.

methods.name: fz_td_recipe.parts.synapse_properties.SynapseProperties._unmatched
documentation: Yields rules that do not match up.

Container to provide access to the synapse classification and properties.

See also the classification rules {class}`~SynapseRules` and classification properties
{class}`~SynapseClasses`.

modules.name: fz_td_recipe.parts.touch_connections
documentation: Parametrization of synapse connectivity.

classes.name: fz_td_recipe.parts.touch_connections.ConnectionRule

methods.name: fz_td_recipe.parts.touch_connections.ConnectionRule.overrides
documentation:
Returns true if the current rule supersedes the `other` one, false otherwise.

The more specialized, i.e., the less `columns` contain the global
match `*`, the higher the precedence of a rule.
I.e. `<rule fromMType="L*" …/>` will override `<rule fromMType="*" …/>`.

methods.name: fz_td_recipe.parts.touch_connections.ConnectionRule.__lt__
documentation: Less specialized rules are smaller, then by attribute.

methods.name: fz_td_recipe.parts.touch_connections.ConnectionRule.validate
documentation: Verifies that every rule uses only allowed attribute combinations.
Class defining parameters to determine reduction and cutting probabilities.

classes.name: fz_td_recipe.parts.touch_connections.ConnectionRules
documentation: Group of `ConnectionRule`.

modules.name: fz_td_recipe.legacy
documentation: Top-level interface for legacy XML recipe handling.

classes.name: fz_td_recipe.legacy.XMLRecipe

methods.name: fz_td_recipe.legacy.XMLRecipe.__init__
documentation: Create a new instance from `path`.

methods.name: fz_td_recipe.legacy.XMLRecipe.validate
documentation:
Validate basic functionality of the recipe.

Checks provided here-in test for basic coverage, and provides a
weak assertion that the recipe should be functional.

The parameter `values` should be a dictionary containing all
allowed values in the form ``fromMType``

methods.name: fz_td_recipe.legacy.XMLRecipe.dump
documentation:
Write the recipe to the provided file descriptors.

If `connectivity_fd` is given, the connection rules of the recipe
are written to it, otherwise dump the whole recipe to `fd`.

methods.name: fz_td_recipe.legacy.XMLRecipe.__str__
documentation: Converts the recipe into XML.

class_variables.name: fz_td_recipe.legacy.XMLRecipe.bouton_interval
documentation:
The interval parametrization specifying how touches should be
distributed where cell morphologies overlap.

class_variables.name: fz_td_recipe.legacy.XMLRecipe.bouton_distances
documentation: Optional definition of the bouton distances from the soma.

class_variables.name: fz_td_recipe.legacy.XMLRecipe.connection_rules
documentation: Optional parameters for the connectivity reduction.

class_variables.name: fz_td_recipe.legacy.XMLRecipe.gap_junction_properties
documentation: Optional definition of attributes for gap junctions.

class_variables.name: fz_td_recipe.legacy.XMLRecipe.touch_reduction
documentation:
Optional parameter for a simple trimming of touches with a survival
probability.

class_variables.name: fz_td_recipe.legacy.XMLRecipe.touch_rules
documentation:
Detailed definition of allowed synapses. All touches not matching
the definitions here will be removed.

class_variables.name: fz_td_recipe.legacy.XMLRecipe.seeds
documentation:
Seeds to use when generating random numbers during the touch
reduction stage.

class_variables.name: fz_td_recipe.legacy.XMLRecipe.spine_lengths
documentation:
Defines percentiles for the length of spines of synapses, i.e., the
distance between the surface positions of touches.

class_variables.name: fz_td_recipe.legacy.XMLRecipe.structural_spine_lengths
documentation: Defines the maximum length of spines used by TouchDetector.

class_variables.name: fz_td_recipe.legacy.XMLRecipe.synapse_properties
documentation:
Classifies synapses and assigns parameters for the physical
properties of the synapses.

class_variables.name: fz_td_recipe.legacy.XMLRecipe.synapse_reposition
documentation:
Definition of re-assignment of somatic synapses to the first axon
segment.

Recipe information used for functionalizing circuits.

Instances of this class can be used to parse the XML description of our
brain circuits, passed in via the parameter `path`.
All parts of the recipe present are extracted and can be accessed via
attributes, where optional parts may be set to `None`.

Args:

: path: path to an XML file to extract the recipe from
  strict: will raise when additional attributes are encountered, on by default

modules.name: fz_td_recipe.transform
documentation: Functionality to convert XML recipes to JSON and to expand to Pandas.

functions.name: fz_td_recipe.transform._snake_case
documentation: Turn the given string into snake case.

functions.name: fz_td_recipe.transform._rename
documentation: Transform the given string into a functionalizer compatible one.

functions.name: fz_td_recipe.transform._is_directional
documentation: Determine if a column name indicates a direction.

functions.name: fz_td_recipe.transform._to_integer_column
documentation: Add `_i` suffix if appropriate - if the key is from a node population.

functions.name: fz_td_recipe.transform._all_specific_directional_keys
documentation: Extracts all directional attributes that are not defaulted.

functions.name: fz_td_recipe.transform._get_property_attributes
documentation: Return attributes value for an XML rule, taking all defaults into account.

methods.name: fz_td_recipe.transform._Pandifier._get_population
documentation: Get a node population from a circuit configuration.

methods.name: fz_td_recipe.transform._Pandifier.convert
documentation: Converts an XML recipe group to a Pandas DataFrame.

methods.name: fz_td_recipe.transform._Pandifier.get_values
documentation: Get all possible enumeration values for columns.

methods.name: fz_td_recipe.transform._Pandifier.framify
documentation:
Convert group into a Pandas DataFrame.

Expands any partial wildcards, i.e., anything but `*`. Columns relating to the
source and target node population will be tanslated to the corresponding values of
their `@library` enumeration.  Wildcards `*` will have the value `-1` assigned.

methods.name: fz_td_recipe.transform._Pandifier.matrify
documentation:
Construct a rule assignment matrix.

Takes a list of rules, determines which columns relating to the source and target
population are of essence (contain more than just general wildcards `*`).  The
enumeration value count in the corresponding node population `@libray` enumeration
then is used to construct a matrix, where the entries in the matrix set depending
on the numerical values of the columns, with wildcards expanded.

Returns a tuple containing the list of columns used (ordered corresponding to the
matrix dimensions) and the matrix itself.

modules.name: fz_td_recipe.property
documentation: Basic components to build XML recipes.

functions.name: fz_td_recipe.property.singleton
documentation:
Decorate a class to allow initializing with an XML element.

Will search for the classes name in the provided element, and load

Args:
    fct:      the callable object to decorate
    implicit: will create the non-existant decorated element with default parameters

To be used when many properties are read at the same time with different default
values compared to the class wide defaults.  The property initialization will copy
a reference to these local defaults.

classes.name: fz_td_recipe.property.PathwayProperty

methods.name: fz_td_recipe.property.PathwayProperty.columns
documentation:
Column names for attributes used in the pathway.

The attributes of the property that indicate a source / target invocation via the
prefixes `from` and `to`.

```pycon
>>> class rule(PathwayProperty):
...     _attributes = {'toMType': '*', 'fromEType': '*', 'something': str}
>>> r = rule(toMType='B*', fromEType='*')
>>> r.columns()
['fromEType', 'toMType']
```

methods.name: fz_td_recipe.property.PathwayProperty.expand
documentation:
Expand the pathway for partial attribute wildcards.

Expand the property given the possible matching attribute values in `values`,
leaving pure wildcards intact.
Returns an iterator over the property with expanded values.

```pycon
>>> class rule(PathwayProperty):
...     _attributes = {'toMType': '*', 'fromEType': '*', 'something': str}
>>> r = rule(toMType='B*', fromEType='*')
>>> list(r.expand({'toMType': ['Foo', 'Bar', 'Baz']}))
[<rule toMType="Bar" fromEType="*" />, <rule toMType="Baz" fromEType="*" />]
>>> r = rule(toMType='*', fromEType='*')
>>> list(r.expand({'toMType': ['Foo', 'Bar', 'Baz']}))
[<rule toMType="*" fromEType="*" />]
```

methods.name: fz_td_recipe.property.PathwayProperty.__call__
documentation:
Expand the pathway for all possible attribute values.

Expand the property given the possible matching attribute values in `values`.
Returns an iterator over dictionaries with the attribute values matched and the
property itself.

```pycon
>>> class rule(PathwayProperty):
...     _attributes = {'toMType': '*', 'fromEType': '*', 'something': str}
>>> r = rule(toMType='B*', fromEType='*')
>>> list(r({'toMType': ['Foo', 'Bar']}))
[({'toMType': 'Bar'}, <rule toMType="B*" fromEType="*" />)]
```
Property that uses pattern matching to define connectivity.

classes.name: fz_td_recipe.property.PathwayPropertyGroup

methods.name: fz_td_recipe.property.PathwayPropertyGroup.validate
documentation:
Basic validation.

Checks that all rules of the group are correct and all values passed are covered.

```pycon
>>> from fz_td_recipe.parts.touch_connections import ConnectionRules
>>> rules = ConnectionRules.load('''
... <blueColumn>
...   <ConnectionRules>
...     <mTypeRule fromMType="*" toMType="B*"
...       bouton_reduction_factor="0" pMu_A="1.0" p_A="1.0" />
...     <mTypeRule fromMType="*" toMType="Bar"
...       bouton_reduction_factor="0" pMu_A="1.0" p_A="1.0" />
...     <mTypeRule fromMType="*" toMType="Bar"
...       toRegion="Spam" bouton_reduction_factor="0" pMu_A="1.0" p_A="1.0" />
...   </ConnectionRules>
... </blueColumn>
... ''')
>>> values = {
...     'toMType': ['Bar', 'Baz'],
...     'toRegion': ['Ham', 'Eggs', 'Spam']
... }
>>> rules.validate(values)
True
>>> values = {
...     'toMType': ['Foo', 'Bar', 'Baz'],
...     'toRegion': ['Ham', 'Eggs', 'Spam']
... }
>>> rules.validate(values)  # Foo is not covered by the rules!
False
```

methods.name: fz_td_recipe.property.PathwayPropertyGroup.__delitem__
documentation: Delete self[key].

methods.name: fz_td_recipe.property.PathwayPropertyGroup.__getitem__
documentation: Returns the property corresponding to key.

documentation: Remove all items from group.

documentation: Extend group by appending rules from the iterable.

documentation: Insert rule before index.

Remove and return rule at index (default last).

Raises ValueError if the value is not present.

methods.name: fz_td_recipe.property.PathwayPropertyGroup.remove
documentation:
Remove first occurrence of value.

Raises ValueError if the value is not present.

methods.name: fz_td_recipe.property.PathwayPropertyGroup.columns
documentation: The columns of all properties in the list.

methods.name: fz_td_recipe.property.PathwayPropertyGroup.required
documentation:
The required attributes for this property group.

Returns a list of attributes whose values are required for properly selecting
rules, i.e., all attributes used by the enclosed properties, excluding any that
are consistently set to `"*"`.

For example:

```pycon
>>> from fz_td_recipe.parts.touch_connections import ConnectionRules
>>> ConnectionRules.load('''
... <blueColumn>
...   <ConnectionRules>
...     <mTypeRule fromMType="*" toMType="B*" />
...     <mTypeRule fromMType="*" toMType="Bar" />
...     <mTypeRule fromMType="*" toMType="Bar" toRegion="Spam" />
...   </ConnectionRules>
... </blueColumn>
... ''').required
['toMType', 'toRegion']
```

methods.name: fz_td_recipe.property.PathwayPropertyGroup.pathway_index
documentation:
Create a pathway index from `key`.

Generate an integer pathway identifier for the provided `key`, and reference
`values` for all attributes given by {py:attr}`~required`.

```pycon
>>> from fz_td_recipe.parts.touch_connections import ConnectionRules
>>> rules = ConnectionRules.load('''
... <blueColumn>
...   <ConnectionRules>
...     <mTypeRule fromMType="*" toMType="B*" />
...     <mTypeRule fromMType="*" toMType="Bar" />
...     <mTypeRule fromMType="*" toMType="Bar" toRegion="Spam" />
...   </ConnectionRules>
... </blueColumn>
... ''')
>>> values = {
...     'toMType': ['Bar', 'Baz'],
...     'toRegion': ['Ham', 'Eggs', 'Spam']
... }
>>> rules.pathway_index(
...     key={'toMType': 'Bar', 'toRegion': 'Spam'},
...     values=values
... )
4
>>> rules.pathway_index(
...     key={'toMType': 'Baz', 'toRegion': 'Spam'},
...     values=values
... )
5
>>> rules.pathway_index(
...     key={'toMType': 'Baz', 'toRegion': 'Eggs'},
...     values=values
... )
3
```

methods.name: fz_td_recipe.property.PathwayPropertyGroup.pathway_values
documentation:
Resolve the attribute values from pathway index `key`.

Generate a dictionaly of attributes and values that need to be set given an
integer pathway `key` and reference `values` for all attributes given by
{py:attr}`~required`.

```pycon
>>> from fz_td_recipe.parts.touch_connections import ConnectionRules
>>> rules = ConnectionRules.load('''
... <blueColumn>
...   <ConnectionRules>
...     <mTypeRule fromMType="*" toMType="B*" />
...     <mTypeRule fromMType="*" toMType="Bar" />
...     <mTypeRule fromMType="*" toMType="Bar" toRegion="Spam" />
...   </ConnectionRules>
... </blueColumn>
... ''')
>>> values = {
...     'toMType': ['Bar', 'Baz'],
...     'toRegion': ['Ham', 'Eggs', 'Spam']
... }
>>> rules.pathway_values(
...     key=4,
...     values=values
... )
{'toMType': 'Bar', 'toRegion': 'Spam'}
```

methods.name: fz_td_recipe.property.PathwayPropertyGroup.to_pandas
documentation:
Convert the property list to as a Pandas DataFrame.

Any directional attribute will be converted to an integer representation,
corresponding to the index within the map in `values`. The integer columns will
have a `_i` suffix. Any generic wildcard in the form of just `*` will be assigned
a value of -1.

The passed `values` will also be used to expand any *partial* wildcards in the
properties. The corresponding property will be repeated for all matching values.

Columns that consist of purely -1 will be dropped.

```pycon
>>> from fz_td_recipe.parts.touch_connections import ConnectionRules
>>> rules = ConnectionRules.load('''
... <blueColumn>
...   <ConnectionRules>
...     <mTypeRule fromMType="*" toMType="B*" />
...     <mTypeRule fromMType="*" toMType="Bar" />
...     <mTypeRule fromMType="*" toMType="Bar" toRegion="Spam" />
...   </ConnectionRules>
... </blueColumn>
... ''')
>>> values = {
...     'toMType': ['Bar', 'Baz'],
...     'toRegion': ['Ham', 'Eggs', 'Spam']
... }
>>> df = rules.to_pandas(values)
>>> len(df)
4
>>> list(df["toMType_i"])
[0, 1, 0, 0]
>>> list(df["toRegion_i"])
[-1, -1, -1, 2]
>>> "fromRegion_i" in df.columns
False
```
pylint: disable=arguments-differ

methods.name: fz_td_recipe.property.PathwayPropertyGroup.to_matrix
documentation:
Construct a flattened connection rule matrix.

Requires being passed a full set of allowed parameter values passed
as argument `values`. Will raise a {class}`KeyError` if any
required attributes listed by {py:attr}`~required` are not
present.

Returns a {class}`numpy.array` filled with indices into the
{class}`PathwayPropertyGroup` for each possible pathway.
Values greater than the last index of the
{class}`~PathwayPropertyGroup` signify no match with any rule.

```pycon
>>> from fz_td_recipe.parts.touch_connections import ConnectionRules
>>> rules = ConnectionRules.load('''
... <blueColumn>
...   <ConnectionRules>
...     <mTypeRule fromMType="*" toMType="B*" />
...     <mTypeRule fromMType="*" toMType="Bar" />
...     <mTypeRule fromMType="*" toMType="Bar" toRegion="Spam" />
...   </ConnectionRules>
... </blueColumn>
... ''')
>>> values = {
...     'toMType': ['Bar', 'Baz'],
...     'toRegion': ['Ham', 'Eggs', 'Spam']
... }
>>> m = rules.to_matrix(values)
>>> m
array([1, 0, 1, 0, 2, 0], dtype=uint32)
>>> # The last rule matches Bar/Spam
>>> idx = m[rules.pathway_index({'toMType': 'Bar', 'toRegion': 'Spam'}, values)]
>>> idx
np.uint32(2)
>>> rules[idx]
<rule fromMType="*" toMType="Bar" toRegion="Spam" />
```
Property group for `PathwayProperty`.

classes.name: fz_td_recipe.property.MTypeValidator

methods.name: fz_td_recipe.property.MTypeValidator.validate
documentation: Checks that all MType values are covered, and rules match MTypes.

Mixin to validate rules depending on morpholgy types.

Will test both that all rules will match some morphology types, and
that all morphology types passed to `validate` will be covered by at
least one rule.
If the inheriting class has an attribute `_mtype_coverage` set to
`False`, the latter part of the validation will be skipped.
